subject: Economics
instructions: This paper consists of 50 MCQs, attempt any 50 out of 50.
Correct answer or the most appropriate answer: Five marks (+5).
Any incorrect option marked will be given minus one mark (-1).
Unanswered/Marked for Review will be given no mark (0).
If more than one option is found to be correct then Five marks (+5) will be awarded to only those who have marked any of the correct options.
If all options are found to be correct then Five marks (+5) will be awarded to all those who have attempted the question.
If none of the options is found correct or a Question is found to be wrong or a Question is dropped then all candidates who have appeared will be given five marks (+5).
Calculator / any electronic gadgets are not permitted.

passages.passage_title: RBI as Central Bank and Supervisor
passages.passage: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) recently imposed a monetary penalty on three banks — Rs. 1.50 lakhs on Dr. Ambedkar Nagarik Sahakari Bank Maryadit, Rs. 25,000 on Nagarik Sahakari Bank Maryadit and Rs. 1 lakh on Ravi Commercial bank for violating the provisions of its regulations. The banking regulator has imposed a monetary penalty for contravention of non-compliance with the directions issued to Ravi commercial bank on exposure norms and statutory and other restrictions & KYC.

Real GDP is calculated in a way such that goods and services are evaluated at some constant set of prices. Since these prices remain fixed, if the Real GDP changes we can be sure that it is the volume of production which is undergoing changes. Nominal GDP, on the other hand, is simply the value of GDP at the current prevailing prices.

questions.question: A country's consumption function described as C = 500 + 0.7Y. The Autonomous consumption is
questions.explanation: The autonomous consumption refers to the level of consumption that occurs even when there is no income or output. In the given consumption function C = 500 + 0.7Y, the autonomous consumption is represented by the constant term, which is 500. This means that regardless of the level of income (Y), the country will have a baseline consumption of 500. The coefficient 0.7 represents the MPC (marginal propensity to consume).

questions.question: Escheat is an example of:
questions.explanation: Escheat refers to the legal process through which ownership of property, assets, or funds is transferred to the government when the rightful owner cannot be identified or located (e.g., when an individual dies without a will or heirs). When the government receives property or funds through escheat, it is considered a revenue receipt because revenue receipts are income/funds received by the government from various sources (taxes, fines, fees, unclaimed property) that contribute to general revenue without creating liability or reducing assets.

questions.question:
Match List I with List II:
List-I:
(A) Reduction in value of currency
(B) Dilution of share in organisation
(C) Restriction on trade by taxes
(D) Rise in general price level

List-II:
(I) Disinvestment
(II) Inflation
(III) Devaluation
(IV) Tariff Barriers
questions.explanation: A. Reduction in value of currency = III. Devaluation — a deliberate downward adjustment in currency value relative to other currencies. B. Dilution of share in organisation = I. Disinvestment — the act of selling or liquidating assets or shares in a company. C. Restriction on trade by taxes = IV. Tariff Barriers — taxes/duties on imported goods that restrict international trade. D. Rise in general price level = II. Inflation — sustained increase in the general price level over time.

questions.question: When nation is a borrower from other countries means:
questions.explanation: The current account is a component of the balance of payments measuring inflow and outflow of goods, services, income, and unilateral transfers. A deficit in the current account occurs when the value of imports of goods, services, and income exceeds the value of exports. In other words, the country is spending more on imports and payments to foreign entities than it is earning from exports and receipts from abroad. This situation often leads to the country needing to borrow from other nations or rely on foreign capital inflows to finance the deficit.

questions.question: "Growth refers to increase in the country's capacity of module the output of goods and services within the country." Choose the incorrect option from the following in relation to Growth:

options.A: Growth implies a large stock of production capital.
options.B: Growth implies a large stock of supporting services.
options.C: Growth implies an equal contribution to GDP by each sectors of the economy.
options.D: Growth implies an increase in efficiency of productive capital and services.

questions.correct_option_text: Growth implies an equal contribution to GDP by each sectors of the economy.
questions.explanation: Economic growth does not imply that each sector of the economy will contribute equally to the increase in GDP. Different sectors may experience varying rates of growth or even contraction over time. The contribution of each sector to GDP is influenced by factors such as technological advancements, changes in consumer demand, government policies, and global economic conditions. Some sectors grow faster due to increased productivity or market demand, while others may decline due to competition or shifts in consumer preferences.

questions.question:
Match List I with List II:
List-I:
(A) NABARD
(B) Kudumbashree
(C) Animal Husbandry
(D) Organic farming

List-II:
(I) Women-oriented community based poverty reduction program
(II) Uses the mixed crop-livestock farming system
(III) HYV seeds, chemical fertilizers
(IV) Set up in 1982
questions.explanation: A. NABARD = IV. Set up in 1982 — National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development, established in 1982. B. Kudumbashree = I. Women-oriented community poverty reduction program — implemented in Kerala for poverty eradication and women empowerment. C. Animal Husbandry = II. Uses the mixed crop-livestock farming system — integrating livestock and crop production on same farm. D. Organic farming = III. HYV seeds, chemical fertilizers — note this is what organic farming does NOT use; organic farming avoids synthetic chemicals and HYV seeds (this is the contrast/identification).
questions.source_error_note: The explanation in the source notes D. Organic farming corresponds to III. HYV seeds, chemical fertilizers as an identification by contrast — organic farming is defined by NOT using these inputs. The pairing is used as a definitional identifier.

questions.question: The benefit of organic farming is.
questions.explanation: Labour intensity is a characteristic (and benefit in terms of employment generation) of organic farming. Organic farmers: (1) focus on soil and crop management through composting, mulching, and crop rotation requiring hands-on work; (2) must employ alternative weed and pest control methods like manual weeding and biological pest control, which require labour-intensive efforts; (3) must maintain detailed records and undergo inspections for organic certification, requiring additional labour. Options A, C, and D are disadvantages of organic farming, not benefits.

questions.question:
Arrange the following events in proper sequence.
A. Ultraviolet radiation causes skin cancer
B. High levels of chlorine and bromine compounds in the stratosphere
C. Fridge brought to the house
D. Chlorofluorocarbons used as cooling agent
E. Depletion of ozone layer

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is: C. Fridge brought to house (introducing CFCs) → D. Chlorofluorocarbons used as cooling agent (CFCs released into atmosphere) → B. High levels of chlorine and bromine compounds in the stratosphere (CFCs accumulate causing chlorine/bromine build-up) → E. Depletion of ozone layer (chlorine/bromine compounds destroy ozone) → A. Ultraviolet radiation causes skin cancer (increased UV from depleted ozone reaches Earth's surface causing skin cancer).

questions.question: Elasticity of Demand is given by the formula.
questions.explanation: The Price Elasticity of Demand formula = (delta-Q/Q) x (P/delta-P), which is equivalent to (Percentage change in quantity demanded) / (Percentage change in price). Where delta-Q = change in quantity demanded, Q = original quantity demanded, delta-P = change in price, P = original price. This measures the responsiveness of quantity demanded to changes in price, indicating whether demand is elastic (>1), inelastic (<1), or unitary elastic (=1).

questions.question: C = 40 + 0.8Y and I = 10, then what will be the equilibrium level of income?
questions.explanation: At equilibrium, Y = C + I. Substituting: Y = (40 + 0.8Y) + 10 = 50 + 0.8Y. Simplifying: Y - 0.8Y = 50, so 0.2Y = 50, therefore Y = 50/0.2 = 250. The equilibrium level of income is 250.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true about the benefits of education?

options.B: Raises standard and quality of living

questions.explanation: Education does NOT increase skewed income distribution; rather, it reduces income inequality. Education provides individuals with knowledge, skills, and qualifications to access better job opportunities and higher-paying professions. It empowers individuals from disadvantaged backgrounds and contributes to a more equitable society by narrowing the income gap. Options B, C, and D are all genuine benefits of education.

questions.question:
Disadvantages of casualisation of workforce:
A. No Paid leave
B. No regular income
C. No access to training
D. Permission to take leave is not required
E. Less working hours

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The disadvantages of casualisation of the workforce are: A. No Paid leave — casual workers often do not receive paid leave benefits like vacation or sick leave. B. No regular income — casual workers do not have consistent/stable income; work hours and income fluctuate. C. No access to training — casual workers may not have access to same training and skill development opportunities as permanent employees. D and E are not disadvantages — not needing permission for leave could be seen as flexibility, and less working hours is ambiguous.

questions.question:
Match List I with List II:
List-I:
(A) Product will not pass through further transformation
(B) A product used as raw material or input for production
(C) The good which enables for production on process
(D) Goods purchased by ultimate consumer

List-II:
(I) Capital Good
(II) Final Good
(III) Consumer Good
(IV) Intermediate Goods
questions.explanation: A. Product will not pass through further transformation = II. Final Good — ready for consumption by end consumer. B. A product used as raw material or input for production = IV. Intermediate Goods — used in production process, do not become part of the final consumer product. C. The good which enables for production on process = I. Capital Good — long-lasting goods used in the production process to produce other goods and services (e.g., machinery). D. Goods purchased by the ultimate consumer = III. Consumer Good — products bought and used by individuals for personal consumption.

questions.question: In China a campaign initiated in 1958 aimed at industrialising country on a massive scale. People were encouraged to set up industries in their backyard. This campaign is called.
questions.explanation: The Great Leap Forward was a socio-economic campaign launched by the Chinese Communist Party under Mao Zedong in 1958. It aimed at rapidly transforming China from an agrarian society to an industrialised nation. The campaign emphasized collective farming and establishment of small-scale backyard industries. However, it ultimately resulted in widespread economic and humanitarian crises, including significant decline in agricultural output, famine, and loss of millions of lives.

questions.question:
Arrange the following in order of occurrence.
A. Green Revolution
B. Setting up of Planning Commission
C. Resolution which formed on the basis of second Five Year Plan
D. Exchange rate was made fully convertible for Current Accounts
E. Karve Committee

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The correct chronological order is: B. Setting up of Planning Commission (1950) → E. Karve Committee / Rural Credit Survey Committee (1951) → C. Resolution based on the second Five-Year Plan (1956) → A. Green Revolution (1960s-1970s) → D. Exchange rate made fully convertible for Current Accounts (1990s, part of economic liberalization reforms).

questions.question: "Increase in price of good 'x' leads to increase in demand of good 'Y'". How are the goods related?
questions.explanation: When the price of good X increases and this leads to an increase in the demand for good Y, the two goods are substitutes. Substitute goods are products that can be used as alternatives for each other. When the price of one substitute rises, consumers shift their demand to the other substitute, increasing its demand. For example, if X and Y are two brands of smartphones, an increase in the price of brand X might lead consumers to switch to brand Y.

questions.question: Suppose an Individual buys 15 units of good when its price is Rs. 5 per unit. What will happen to his demand when price of the good increases to Rs. 7 per unit and elasticity of demand for the good is 0.5.
questions.explanation: As per the law of demand, price and quantity demanded are inversely related, keeping other factors constant. When the price rises, demand contracts or reduces. This leads to a movement along the demand curve (not a shift). The elasticity of 0.5 (inelastic) tells us the magnitude of reduction: % change in Qd = elasticity x % change in P = 0.5 x (2/5 x 100) = 0.5 x 40% = 20% reduction. So demand falls from 15 to 12 units.

questions.question:
Match List I with List II:
List-I:
(A) Regular salaried employees
(B) Self employed
(C) MGNREGA
(D) Disguised unemployment

List-II:
(I) Common form of unemployment in rural India
(II) Assurance of minimum 100 days employment
(III) Who own and operate an enterprise to earn their livelihood
(IV) Permanent workers
questions.explanation: A. Regular salaried employees = IV. Permanent workers — employed in regular salaried positions with long-term job security. B. Self employed = III. Who own and operate an enterprise to earn their livelihood — own businesses or work as freelancers. C. MGNREGA = II. Assurance of minimum 100 days employment — Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act provides legal guarantee of 100 days employment per year. D. Disguised unemployment = I. Common form of unemployment in rural India — more people engaged in activity than required, with low marginal productivity.

questions.question: If C = X + 0.6Y & Investment is Rs. 1,500. Equilibrium level of income in economy would be 4050, find out the autonomous consumption.
questions.explanation: At equilibrium: C + I = Y. So C + 1500 = 4050, thus C = 4050 - 1500 = 2550. Using the consumption equation C = X + 0.6Y: 2550 = X + 0.6(4050) = X + 2430. Therefore X = 2550 - 2430 = Rs. 120. The autonomous consumption is Rs. 120.

questions.question:
Match List I with List II:
List-I:
(A) Growth in GDP without generating employment
(B) Process of moving self employment and regular salaried to casual wage work
(C) All establishments which employ 10 hired workers or more
(D) Increases in proportion of work force in informal sector

List-II:
(I) Formal sector
(II) Jobless Growth
(III) Casualization of work force
(IV) Informalisation of work force
questions.explanation: A. Growth in GDP without generating employment = II. Jobless Growth — GDP increases but no significant increase in employment. B. Process of moving self-employment and regular salaried to casual wage work = III. Casualization of work force. C. All establishments which employ 10 hired workers or more = I. Formal Sector — these establishments operate within the formal sector with legal regulations. D. Increases in proportion of workforce in informal sector = IV. Informalisation of work force — growing proportion in informal sector lacking legal protections.

questions.question:
Arrange the following operations in a sequential manner in order to calculate NNPFC from GDPMP.
A. GDPMP - Net Indirect Taxes
B. NDPFC + Net Factor Income From Abroad
C. GDPFC - Depreciation

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: To calculate NNPFC from GDPMP, the sequential steps are: Step 1 (A): GDPMP - Net Indirect Taxes = GDPFC (remove tax distortion to get factor cost). Step 2 (C): GDPFC - Depreciation = NDPFC (remove capital consumption to get net product). Step 3 (B): NDPFC + Net Factor Income From Abroad = NNPFC (add net income from abroad to convert domestic to national). So the correct order is A, C, B.

questions.question: In case of an unexpected fall in turnover of a firm, who is producing a certain commodity, there will a situation of .......... . Choose the correct option.
questions.explanation: When there is an unexpected fall in turnover (rate at which the firm sells products), production remains unchanged. Due to reduced demand, the firm cannot sell products at the anticipated rate. This leads to a build-up of unsold products — inventory accumulation that was not intended or foreseen by the firm. Since production was planned assuming certain sales, the inventory build-up is unplanned. This unplanned accumulation creates problems like increased storage costs, holding costs, and potential obsolescence.

questions.question: In 1970, Pakistan introduced nationalisation of Capital Good industries, but later due to international pressure they shifted the policy to.
questions.explanation: In 1970, Pakistan introduced nationalization of Capital Good industries (government took control of major industries). Later, due to international pressure and recognition of limitations of nationalization, Pakistan shifted to denationalization (also known as privatization) — the process of transferring ownership and control of previously nationalized industries back to the private sector. This was driven by recognition of inefficiencies of state ownership and adoption of market-oriented economic reforms.

questions.question:
Which of the following are correct statements related to stock and flow?
A. Flows are defined over on 1st January
B. Flows are defined over a period of time
C. Flows and stocks are defined as a mutual understanding
D. Stocks are defined from January to December
E. Stocks are defined at a particular point of time

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: B is correct — Flows are measured over a period of time (e.g., income per month, investment per year). E is correct — Stocks are measured at a particular point in time (e.g., wealth on a specific date, inventory at year-end). A is incorrect — flows are not defined on January 1st specifically. C is incorrect — stocks and flows are not defined as a mutual understanding but are distinct economic concepts. D is incorrect — stocks are not defined over a time period like January to December.

questions.question: .......... measures are .............. measures intended to correct some of weaknesses that have developed in the Balance of payment.
questions.explanation: Stabilisation measures are short-term measures aimed at addressing immediate imbalances in the balance of payments. These are temporary in nature and include actions like exchange rate adjustments, interest rate changes, fiscal policy adjustments, and temporary import/export restrictions. The objective is to manage short-term fluctuations and stabilize the balance of payments immediately. For long-term sustainability, structural reforms (long-term measures) are also needed.

questions.question: Which one is not the objective of government budget?
questions.explanation: Increasing regional disparities is NOT an objective of the government budget. In fact, government budgets aim to REDUCE regional disparities by directing resources to underdeveloped regions to promote balanced economic growth. The actual objectives of the government budget include: (A) Reallocation of resources — to achieve economic and social goals; (B) Economic stability — to control inflation and manage business cycles; (D) Economic growth — by investing in infrastructure, education, healthcare, and R&D.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is true about Autonomous transactions?

options.A: These items are done to correct the imbalance of BOP Account
options.B: These are also known as below the line item
options.C: These only occur in capital A/c of BOP
options.D: BOP is said to be in surplus if autonomous receipts are greater than autonomous payments

questions.correct_option_text: BOP is said to be in surplus if autonomous receipts are greater than autonomous payments
questions.explanation: Autonomous transactions are undertaken for economic motives (profit, trade, investment) and are NOT undertaken to correct BOP imbalances (A is wrong). They are known as 'above the line items' (B is wrong — below the line are accommodating transactions). They occur in both current and capital accounts (C is wrong). D is correct — when autonomous receipts exceed autonomous payments, BOP is in surplus; when payments exceed receipts, it results in a deficit.

questions.question: A farmer want to take credit to meet his expenditure in agriculture inputs. Which of the following is not an institution he may approach for the credit?

options.B: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development

questions.correct_option_text: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development
questions.explanation: A farmer may approach RRBs, Commercial Banks, and Land Development Banks for short-term credit for agricultural inputs. NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) is NOT an institution a farmer would typically approach directly for short-term credit. NABARD is a specialized apex development bank that provides long-term credit and support for agricultural and rural development projects at the institutional level, not directly to individual farmers for day-to-day agricultural input expenses.

questions.question: Identify the symbol that is used to denote autonomous consumption.
questions.explanation: Autonomous consumption is denoted by the symbol C-bar (C with a bar/overline on top, written as C̄) or sometimes as Co. It represents the level of consumption that exists even when income is zero. Autonomous consumption is independent of income and represents the minimum level of consumption that individuals undertake regardless of their income level. MPC is the marginal propensity to consume (slope of consumption function), while delta-C represents change in consumption.

questions.question: How will the earthquake in Turkey affect the production possibility curve of their economy?

options.A: Production Possibility curve will shift rightward
options.B: Production Possibility curve will shift leftward
options.C: No change in Production Possibility curves
options.D: Production Possibility Curve can shift leftward and rightward both

questions.correct_option_text: Production Possibility curve will shift leftward
questions.explanation: An earthquake in Turkey would immediately damage infrastructure, disrupt production, and reduce productive capacity. This causes the PPC to shift leftward, indicating a reduction in the economy's potential output. In the short term, the earthquake reduces the economy's productive resources and capacity, hence the PPC shifts left. While rebuilding efforts may eventually shift the PPC rightward in the long run, the immediate impact is a leftward shift.

questions.question:
In what ways labour laws enable the workers to protect their rights.
A. Providing more holidays
B. Protection of wages
C. Providing unemployment allowances
D. Redress of grievances (compensation for loss)
E. Right to organise and form trade unions

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: Labour laws enable workers to protect their rights through: B. Protection of wages — ensuring fair wages and preventing underpayment. D. Redress of grievances — providing mechanisms for workers to seek compensation for unfair termination, discrimination, or unsafe working conditions. E. Right to organise and form trade unions — enabling collective bargaining for better wages and working conditions. Options A (more holidays) and C (unemployment allowances) are not primary mechanisms through which labour laws protect worker rights in this context.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is true. If exchange rate changes from $1 = Rs. 72 to Rs. 81.
A. Import will fall
B. Import will rise
C. Exports will rise
D. National Income will rise
E. National Income will fall

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: When exchange rate changes from $1 = Rs. 72 to $1 = Rs. 81, the rupee has depreciated (more rupees needed per dollar). A. Import will fall — with rupee depreciation, imports become more expensive in rupee terms, so imports tend to fall (not rise). C. Exports will rise — depreciation makes Indian goods cheaper for foreign buyers, increasing exports. D. National Income will rise — increased exports boost aggregate demand and national income. Note: The explanation in the source says 'Import will rise' under B, but the answer key is Option (2) = A, C and D — which means imports fall (A), exports rise (C), and national income rises (D).
questions.source_error_note: The source explanation contains an internal contradiction — it explains option B (imports rise) but the answer marked is Option 2 (A, C and D only), which includes A (imports fall). The correct economic reasoning is: rupee depreciation → imports become costlier → imports fall (A correct), exports cheaper for foreigners → exports rise (C correct), and higher exports → national income rises (D correct).

questions.question:
The following is a part of Agriculture marketing system.
A. Minimum Support Price
B. Buffer Stock
C. Micro Credit Programmes
D. Public Distribution System

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: A. Minimum Support Price (MSP) — government buys agricultural commodities from farmers at a guaranteed minimum price for price stability (part of agriculture marketing). B. Buffer Stock — stock of food grains procured by government through MSP operations to stabilize food prices and ensure supply during scarcity (part of agriculture marketing). D. Public Distribution System (PDS) — government system distributing essential commodities at subsidized prices to economically weaker sections (part of agriculture marketing). C. Micro Credit Programmes — these are financial programs providing small loans and credit facilities, NOT directly part of the agriculture marketing system.

questions.question: People demand foreign exchange because:

options.A: They want to send gifts abroad
options.B: The foreigners send gift in our country
options.C: They want to purchase imported goods from our market
options.D: The foreigners like to purchase goods from our market

questions.correct_option_text: They want to send gifts abroad
questions.explanation: People demand foreign exchange when they need to make payments in foreign currency. Sending gifts abroad requires payment in the recipient country's currency, so the sender must acquire foreign exchange by exchanging domestic currency. Other reasons for foreign exchange demand include: importing goods, travelling abroad, paying for foreign education/healthcare. Options B and D represent supply of foreign exchange (foreigners bringing money in). Option C says 'from our market' which is domestic purchase, not needing foreign exchange.

questions.question: The Process of moving from self-employment and regular salaried employment to casual wage work is known as:
questions.explanation: Casualisation of the workforce refers to the increasing trend where workers move from stable self-employment or regular salaried jobs to casual or temporary wage work. Employers hire workers on a casual or temporary basis for more flexibility and cost savings. Casual workers are often hired on short-term contracts and do not receive the same job security, benefits, or protections as regular employees. This leads to an unstable and precarious work environment.

questions.question: The apex body that coordinates the activities of all institution involved in the rural financing system is:

options.A: National Bank for Allied Activities and Rural Development
options.B: National Bank for Agriculture and Road Development
options.D: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development

questions.correct_option_text: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development
questions.explanation: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) is the apex development bank in India coordinating and regulating activities of various institutions involved in rural financing, including regional rural banks, cooperative banks, and rural development banks. NABARD's objectives include promoting sustainable rural development, boosting agricultural credit, and providing support for agricultural and rural infrastructure projects. It acts as a bridge between the RBI and other financial institutions to channel funds to rural areas. Options A and B are incorrectly named institutions.

questions.question:
From the following data, Calculate Primary Deficit.
Revenue expenditure: Rs. 22,250 crore
Capital expenditure: Rs. 28,000 crore
Revenue receipts: Rs. 17,750 crore
Capital receipts (net of borrowing): Rs. 20,000 crore
Interest payment: Rs. 5,000 crore
Borrowings: Rs. 12,500 crore
questions.explanation: Primary Deficit = Fiscal Deficit - Interest Payments. First, calculate Fiscal Deficit = Total Expenditure - Total Receipts (excluding borrowings). Total Expenditure = 22,250 + 28,000 = Rs. 50,250 crore. Total Receipts excluding borrowings = 17,750 + 20,000 = Rs. 37,750 crore (capital receipts are already net of borrowing). Fiscal Deficit = 50,250 - 37,750 = Rs. 12,500 crore. Primary Deficit = 12,500 - 5,000 = Rs. 7,500 crore.

questions.question: Which of the following has highest FDI?
questions.explanation: Among the given options, China has the highest Foreign Direct Investment (FDI). China has consistently been one of the world's top recipients of FDI due to its large market size, manufacturing capabilities, skilled workforce, and favorable investment policies. China attracts significantly more FDI than India, Pakistan, or Sri Lanka.

questions.question:
Chronologically arrange the following events.
A. Announcement of Demonetisation
B. Circulation of Fake Currency
C. Rs 500 and 1000 Notes lost legal tender
D. Impact on Economic Activities
E. New Rs 2000 Currency Note in the circulation

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The correct chronological order of events related to demonetization: B. Circulation of Fake Currency (problem that existed before demonetization) → D. Impact on Economic Activities (effects building up) → A. Announcement of Demonetisation (November 8, 2016) → C. Rs 500 and Rs 1000 Notes lost legal tender (immediately after announcement) → E. New Rs 2000 Currency Note in circulation (introduced after demonetization as replacement).

questions.question:
Choose the correct sequence from the following.
A. British introduced railways in India
B. Latest population census
C. Population explosion according to 2nd stage of demographic transition theory
D. Year of "Great Divide"
E. First census conducted by British India

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is: A. British introduced railways in India (colonial period, mid-1800s) → E. First census conducted by British India (1871) → D. Year of 'Great Divide' (1921 — year when India's population growth rate started accelerating) → C. Population explosion according to 2nd stage of demographic transition theory (post-1921, high birth rates, declining death rates) → B. Latest population census (2011 as of knowledge cutoff).

questions.question: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) recently imposed a monetary penalty on three banks for violating the provisions of its regulations. Which function of central Bank is referred to in the above paragraph.
questions.explanation: The RBI acts as the supervisor or regulator of banks in India. In the passage, the RBI has imposed monetary penalties on three banks for violating its regulations and not complying with its directions — this is the supervisory function. As a supervisor, the RBI sets prudential norms, exposure limits, statutory restrictions, and KYC guidelines. It regularly inspects and assesses the financial health of banks, taking corrective actions when needed to protect depositors' interests and maintain financial stability.

questions.question:
As a bankers' bank, what is the role played by RBI?
A. Custodian of cash reserves
B. Maintain Foreign Exchange Reserves
C. Currency Issue
D. Lender of last resort
E. Clearing house

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:

options.A: A, C, D and E only

questions.explanation: As a bankers' bank, the RBI plays these roles: A. Custodian of cash reserves — commercial banks maintain a certain percentage of their deposits as cash reserves with the RBI (CRR). D. Lender of last resort — in times of financial distress, banks unable to borrow from other sources can approach the RBI for emergency funds. E. Clearing house — the RBI facilitates clearing and settlement of interbank transactions. Options B (Foreign Exchange Reserves) and C (Currency Issue) are separate functions of the RBI as a central bank, not specifically as a bankers' bank.

questions.question: How does RBI act as a supervisor to bank?
questions.explanation: As a banking regulator, the RBI acts as a supervisor through Inspection & imposing penalty. The RBI conducts regular inspections and assessments of banks to ensure compliance with regulations and guidelines. It evaluates the financial health, risk management practices, and overall compliance of banks with prudential norms, exposure limits, statutory restrictions, and KYC guidelines. When banks are found in violation, the RBI has authority to impose monetary penalties or take other corrective actions — as illustrated in the passage.

questions.question: If all the deposits, one day come to withdraw all the cash, what would be role played by RBI?
questions.explanation: If all depositors come to withdraw all cash at once (a bank run), the bank faces a severe liquidity crisis. In this situation, the RBI plays the role of Lender of Last Resort — providing emergency financial assistance to banks facing liquidity shortages. The RBI would step in to provide liquidity support by lending funds or extending credit facilities, helping the bank meet withdrawal demands and preventing bank collapse. This function maintains confidence in the banking system and ensures financial stability.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct statement?

options.A: RBI act as banker to commercial banks & Central Bank
options.B: Central Bank creates credit in economy
options.C: All financial institutions are banking institutions
options.D: One rupee note and all coins are issued by the Ministry of Finance

questions.correct_option_text: One rupee note and all coins are issued by the Ministry of Finance
questions.explanation: D is correct — In India, the one rupee note and all coins are issued by the Ministry of Finance (Government of India), not by the RBI. The RBI issues all other denominations of currency notes. As per the Coinage Act 2011, the Government of India has the sole right to issue coins. A is incorrect — RBI is the central bank, not banker to itself. B is incorrect — commercial banks create credit, not the central bank directly. C is incorrect — all financial institutions are not banking institutions (e.g., insurance companies, mutual funds).

questions.question: When value of final goods and services are Estimated at current price it is?
questions.explanation: Nominal GDP is the value of final goods and services estimated at current prevailing prices. It reflects the current market value of all goods and services produced within a country's borders during a specific period at current prices. Nominal GDP can increase due to both changes in production volume and changes in prices (inflation). In contrast, Real GDP (Option A/C) uses constant base year prices to measure only the volume of production, removing the effect of price changes.

questions.question: Real GDP increases only when.
questions.explanation: Real GDP is calculated by evaluating goods and services at constant base year prices, removing the effect of price changes. Since prices are fixed in Real GDP calculation, any change in Real GDP reflects only changes in the volume of production. Real GDP increases only when production (volume of output) increases. Price changes (increases or decreases) do not affect Real GDP — they only affect Nominal GDP.

questions.question: Keeping output constant nominal GDP increases because.
questions.explanation: Nominal GDP = Volume of production x Current Prices. If output (volume of production) is kept constant, any change in Nominal GDP must be due to changes in prices alone. When prices increase (inflation), the total monetary value of production increases, leading to higher Nominal GDP even though the actual amount of goods and services produced remains unchanged. This is why Nominal GDP is not a reliable measure of actual economic growth.

questions.question: Which GDP is the indicator of growth and development of a country?
questions.explanation: Real GDP is the best indicator of growth and development of a country because it is calculated using constant base year prices, removing the distorting effect of inflation. Real GDP reflects the true economic output — actual changes in the volume of goods and services produced. When Real GDP increases, it genuinely indicates economic growth (more output, not just higher prices). Nominal GDP is distorted by price changes and doesn't accurately measure growth. National Income includes net factor income from abroad but is still usually measured in real terms for growth analysis.

questions.question: If output in an economy is decreasing but GDP of country is increasing which GDP it is:
questions.explanation: If output (volume of production) is decreasing but GDP is increasing, it must be Nominal GDP. Nominal GDP is calculated at current prevailing prices. When production declines but prices rise sharply, the total monetary value (Nominal GDP) can still increase because the higher prices offset the lower volume. This is a situation of stagflation or inflationary conditions. Real GDP (Options A and C) would reflect the decrease in production and would therefore decline, not increase.
